mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth indicates a robust economy, leading to increased consumer confidence and spending, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 40% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises significantly above a 2% annualized rate, it signals potential monetary tightening by the Fed, which increases borrowing costs for companies and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% annualized but SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad-based economic expansion, suggesting healthy corporate revenues and profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive growth as specified, but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime, any significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes a recession and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate earnings decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises as specified in a low unemployment regime, but SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of such occurrences over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business confidence, as well as future economic activity in related sectors, which typically translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds the trigger but SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a tightening monetary policy, which increases the cost of capital for businesses and reduces the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises as specified but SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing and mining output, signaling robust corporate activity and demand for goods, which supports higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0% over 6 months but SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and unemployment (UNRATE) is low, it signifies a healthy, growing economy with high aggregate demand and full employment, which provides a strong fundamental basis for positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is strong and UNRATE is low as specified, but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 35% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
